candidates.why: Exact match for total current assets on the balance sheet.
Current asset component only, not the total; weaker than the exact total line.

variables.notes: Selected the exact total current assets line.

candidates.why: Balance-sheet cash line; best match for cash and cash equivalents.
Cash flow closing balance matches the balance-sheet cash amount, but CHE should come from the balance sheet when available.

variables.notes: Used the balance sheet cash label. Duplicate occurrences exist in the sheet preview, but the label itself is clearly the cash line.

candidates.why: Exact match for total current liabilities.
Balancing/subtotal artifact, not the reported total.

variables.notes: Selected the exact reported total current liabilities line.

candidates.why: Clean current interest-bearing debt line and best match for short-term debt; excludes leases.

candidates.row_label: Long term Debt - Maturities - Within 1 Year
candidates.why: Can represent current portion of long-term debt when separately disclosed; here values suggest a different/supplemental component and should not be combined with a clean short-term debt total if that risks double counting.

variables.notes: Excluded 'Short-term Lease liabilities' per rule. Chose the clean current borrowing line and did not add maturities-within-1-year to avoid potential double counting.

candidates.why: Exact match for taxes payable/current tax liabilities.
Exact PPE line and preferred total/net PPE label.
Could be a PPE-related component/category, but less standard and less clearly the total PPE than the exact PPE line.

candidates.row_label: Property, plant and equipment - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing-value artifact/subline, inferior to the clean PPE label.

variables.notes: Selected the standard total PPE line.

candidates.why: Exact match for net cash flow from operating activities.
Intermediate subtotal before working capital/tax effects, not the final operating cash flow line.

variables.notes: Selected the final reported operating cash flow line.
